metadata.title: States PCS: Andhra Pradesh GK For APPSC Examinations

questions.question: Who was appointed as the first Vice-Chancellor of Andhra University in 1926?
questions.explanation: Cattamanchi Ramalinga Reddy became the first Vice-Chancellor of Andhra University in 1926, establishing key academic and administrative frameworks.

questions.question: Who described Kandukuri Veeresalingam as the “South Indian Vidyasagar” in appreciation of his social service activities?
questions.explanation: M.G. Ranade, a Maharashtra social reformer, called Veeresalingam the “South Indian Vidyasagar” for his leading role in widow remarriage and women’s reform in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: On which date did Potti Sreeramulu attain martyrdom for the cause of a separate Andhra State?
questions.explanation: Potti Sreeramulu died on December 15, 1952, after a 56-day hunger strike demanding a separate Andhra State, leading to its creation in 1953 and setting a precedent for linguistic state reorganization.

questions.question: Where and when was the first Rayalaseema Mahasabha conducted?
questions.explanation: The first Rayalaseema Mahasabha was held in Madras on 28 January 1934 to address issues of the four Rayalaseema districts, led by advocates like M. Venkatakrishnaiah and K. Narayana Rao, shaping the Andhra statehood movement.

questions.question: Which tribal community in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana was historically classified under the Criminal Tribes Act?
questions.explanation: Yerukala is a tribal community in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, mainly in southern Coastal Andhra and Rayalaseema. Their native language is Yerukala, but most speak Telugu. Literacy rate is 48.12%. They were historically labeled under the Criminal Tribes Act.

questions.question: Under which brand name does the Andhra Pradesh Dairy Development Cooperative Federation Ltd (APDDCF) operate its dairy value chain?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh Dairy Development Cooperative Federation Ltd (APDDCF), established in 1981, develops the dairy value chain under the VIJAYA brand. Andhra Pradesh ranks 4th in India’s milk production with 24 lakh dairy farmers and 769 Dairy Cooperative Societies.

questions.question: According to Kridabhiraman, what was Orugallu (modern Warangal) renowned for during the Kakatiya dynasty period?

options.label: B
options.text: Major hub for sculpture and architecture

options.label: C
options.text: Prominent center of trade and commerce

options.label: D
options.text: Abode of drama artists and performers

answer.label: D
answer.text: Abode of drama artists and performers

questions.explanation: Kridabhiraman described Orugallu as renowned for its drama artists; Kakatiya kings notably patronized performing arts here, with evidence in historical sites like the Ramappa Temple and Ganapathi Gate.

questions.question: Who is traditionally credited with contributing significantly to the spread of Buddhism during the reign of Virapurushadatta?
questions.explanation: Traditional references attribute the propagation of Buddhism during Virapurushadatta’s reign to Upasika Bodhisri, though there is no epigraphic or archaeological evidence for either figure in Andhra Pradesh’s Buddhist history.

questions.question: Which was the first historically recorded dynasty to rule the Deccan region?
questions.explanation: Satavahanas, originating in present-day Andhra Pradesh in the 1st century BCE, were the earliest historically documented dynasty of the Deccan. They promoted Prakrit language, trade, and Buddhism.

questions.question: Who succeeded Kakati Ganapatideva as the ruler of the Kakatiya dynasty?
questions.explanation: Rudramadevi, daughter of Ganapatideva, was one of the earliest female monarchs in India, ruling Kakatiya dynasty from c. 1259–1295. She defended the kingdom from invasions, promoted irrigation, and supported Telugu culture.
